description: Cannot borrow value as mutable while it is also borrowed as immutable
rationale: Aliasing XOR mutability: a value can have either one mutable reference or any number of immutable references, never both
fix_template: Drop the immutable borrow before creating the mutable borrow
alternatives: Restructure to separate read and write phases

description: Cannot borrow value as mutable more than once at a time
rationale: Multiple mutable references to the same data enable data races and aliasing bugs
fix_template: Scope the first mutable borrow to end before the second begins

description: Cannot borrow value after it has been moved
rationale: Once ownership is transferred, the original binding is invalid and cannot be borrowed
fix_template: Borrow before moving, or clone the value
alternatives: Use a reference instead of moving

description: Collection modified while iterating over it
rationale: Mutating a collection during iteration can invalidate the iterator, causing undefined behavior
fix_template: Collect indices to modify, then apply changes after the loop

description: Unnecessary borrow: value is Copy and could be passed by value
rationale: For small Copy types, passing by value is as efficient as passing by reference and avoids indirection
fix_template: Pass by value instead of by reference

description: Cannot return a reference to a local variable
rationale: Local variables are dropped at the end of the function; returning a reference to them creates a dangling pointer
fix_template: Return the value by ownership instead of by reference
alternatives: Allocate on the heap with Box/Rc/Arc

description: Mutable borrow held across an await point
rationale: Await points suspend execution; holding a mutable borrow across one could allow concurrent access in another task
fix_template: Scope the mutable borrow to end before the await, or clone the data
alternatives: Use a Mutex for cross-await access

description: Borrow used in match arm prevents modification in other arms
rationale: Match arms may need exclusive access; holding borrows across arms constrains what other arms can do
pattern: Match(x) { arm1 => Borrow(&x.field), arm2 => BorrowMut(&mut x.field) }
fix_template: Extract the borrowed value before the match
alternatives: Restructure the match to avoid cross-arm borrows

description: Cannot hold &mut Vec<T> across loop iteration that also reads via &Vec<T>
rationale: Simultaneous mutable and immutable access to a Vec can cause iterator invalidation and data corruption
fix_template: Clone before loop or use index-based access

description: Mutable borrow inside loop may cause reallocation invalidation
rationale: Vec::push() may reallocate the backing buffer, invalidating any existing references to elements
